headline: Lennar’s Land Light Shift Raises Questions On Hidden Costs And Margins
summary: Lennar (NYSE:LEN) is accelerating its shift to a land-light model and increasingly relying on institutional land banking for its communities. This approach introduces recurring option fees and other embedded costs that may not be fully visible in reported earnings. Investors are assessing how these hidden costs could affect Lennar’s margins, cash flows, and resilience in a housing downturn. Lennar’s stock, NYSE:LEN, last closed at $85.25, with the share price down 18.2% year to date and...
